title: Cleistanthus lanceolatus
taxonomy: Cleistanthus lanceolatus is classified in the family Phyllanthaceae. This family, also known as the goosefoot family, is a group of flowering plants. Cleistanthus lanceolatus, along with other members of the family, belongs to the order Malpighiales. The family Phyllanthaceae is part of the larger clade Rosidae, which includes many other plant families. Cleistanthus lanceolatus is also a part of the class Magnoliopsida.
uses: Cleistanthus lanceolatus is known for its use in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. The plant's roots and leaves are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever and infections. In Ayurvedic medicine, Cleistanthus lanceolatus is used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain. Additionally, it is used to treat digestive issues, including diarrhea and dysentery.

intro: Cleistanthus lanceolatus, also known as the Indian laburnum or lance-leaved cleistanthus, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, belonging to the family Phyllanthaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cleistanthus lanceolatus include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cleistanthus lanceolatus is often prepared as a decoction or a powder, and is used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, capsules, and creams, to treat a range of health issues.
